seoTitle: How to Change Your QuickBooks Email Template
metaDescription: Customize QuickBooks email templates for invoices and reminders to keep client communication consistent with your brand.
intro: The emails your business sends through QuickBooks — invoices, estimates, payment reminders — are part of your brand's first impression, so a generic default template is worth customizing. This guide covers navigating to the templates section and adjusting the layout and content. It's aimed at small business owners who want more consistent, professional client communication.

steps.heading: Navigate the email template section
steps.body: Find the settings area in QuickBooks where email templates are managed.

steps.heading: Customize content and layout
steps.body: Edit the wording and structure of the template to match your business's tone.

steps.heading: Brand your emails
steps.body: Add your logo, colors, or consistent messaging so the email matches your other materials.

steps.heading: Set up automated emails
steps.body: Configure the template to send automatically for invoices or payment confirmations where appropriate.

faq.question: Can I have different templates for invoices and reminders?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks typically allows separate templates for different email types like invoices and payment reminders.

faq.question: Will customizing the template affect existing sent emails?
faq.answer: No, changes apply to future emails sent, not ones already delivered.

faq.question: Can I add my logo to the email template?
faq.answer: Yes, branding elements like a logo can typically be added to keep emails consistent with your business identity.
